summary: Virginia M.-Y. Lee reflects on her unconventional career path and the importance of business skills in scientific research. She emphasizes that running a large research lab requires the organizational and financial mindset of a small-business owner.

principles.statement: Running a large scientific lab requires formal business and organizational skills.
principles.rationale: A large lab involves managing finances, paying bills, ordering supplies, and organizing infrastructure for dozens of people, which goes beyond basic scientific training.
principles.source_quote: On the small scale, it’s not as important, but when it’s on the large scale, any kind of business training and organization skills, as well as knowing about finances, is important to running a big lab.

mental_models.name: The Lab as a Mom-and-Pop Shop
mental_models.description: Viewing the management of a scientific research lab through the lens of running a small business, where securing funding, paying bills, and managing supplies are central to daily operations.
mental_models.example: Applying her M.B.A. training to implement infrastructure for a 50-person research team, managing data, banking samples, and running drug-discovery programs.

heuristics: Make career decisions based on where you want to live, what you want to see, and what you want to learn—then just go do it.

signature_quotes.text: Running your own lab is like running a mom-and-pop shop. You get money. You have to pay bills. You have to order supplies and so on.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how her M.B.A. training helps her manage a 50-person research team.

signature_quotes.text: I made decisions based on a number of factors — of where I wanted to go and live, what I wanted to see and what I wanted to learn. Then I just went and did it.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on her unusual career path and lack of a traditional role model.

signature_quotes.text: Science is so exciting now. I would have never imagined, as a high-school girl or even as a postdoc, where I am right now.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on her three decades of research into neurodegenerative diseases.
